name: Da Nang
summary: Da Nang is widely considered one of Vietnam's safest major cities with low crime rates, friendly locals, and excellent tourist infrastructure. The city has a visible police presence, well-lit streets, and organized traffic compared to other Vietnamese cities. Most visitors enjoy trouble-free stays with only minor concerns about typhoons during the seasonal period.
quickVerdict: Da Nang stands out as one of Southeast Asia's safest destinations for tourists and expats alike. With violent crime being extremely rare and petty theft uncommon, the city offers a secure environment for all types of travelers. The US State Department maintains a Level 1 advisory for Vietnam, recommending normal precautions. While the city faces occasional typhoons and minor tourist scams, these risks are manageable with basic awareness. The local government prioritizes tourism security, maintaining regular police patrols and tourist assistance programs.

neighborhoods.name: My Khe Beach
neighborhoods.description: The most popular and safest tourist area with upscale resorts, security guards, CCTV systems, and regular lifeguard presence. Well-lit at night with plenty of restaurants and bars.

neighborhoods.name: An Thuong
neighborhoods.description: Trendy beachside district with vibrant nightlife, cafes, and tourist amenities. Heavy foot traffic and police presence make it ideal for solo female travelers and nighttime activities.

neighborhoods.name: Hai Chau District (Downtown)
neighborhoods.description: The city center with cultural landmarks, markets, and business district. Well-populated with good lighting and frequent police patrols, though can be busy during peak hours.

neighborhoods.name: Son Tra Peninsula
neighborhoods.description: Scenic mountainous area with temples and nature trails. Generally safe during daylight hours but requires caution on winding roads and when hiking alone.

neighborhoods.name: Thanh Khe District
neighborhoods.description: Local residential area with authentic Vietnamese atmosphere. Safe for experienced travelers but may have language barriers and fewer tourist services.

neighborhoods.name: Cam Le District
neighborhoods.description: Quieter suburban area primarily for locals. While not unsafe, it's less developed for tourists with limited English signage and fewer amenities.

scams.name: Airport Taxi Overcharging
scams.description: Unmetered taxi drivers at Da Nang Airport quote inflated prices (up to 10x normal rates) and may show fake Grab prices on their phones to justify high fares.
scams.howToAvoid: Use the official Grab app to check real prices, insist on metered taxis from licensed companies like Mai Linh or Vinasun, or book through your hotel.

scams.name: Motorbike Rental Scams
scams.description: Rental shops may claim fake damage to bikes, hold passports hostage for payment, or even steal back their own motorbikes to force customers to pay again.
scams.howToAvoid: Take detailed photos/videos before renting, never leave your passport as collateral (offer cash deposit), and choose reputable shops recommended by other travelers.

scams.name: Currency Exchange Manipulation
scams.description: Informal money changers offer poor exchange rates or give counterfeit/outdated bills while claiming 'no commission' to attract tourists.
scams.howToAvoid: Use official banks like Vietcombank or BIDV for currency exchange, check current rates online beforehand, and inspect all bills carefully.

scams.name: Fake Tour Agencies
scams.description: Unlicensed operators offer cheap tours with poor service quality, fake itineraries, or hidden fees that surface during the trip.
scams.howToAvoid: Book through licensed agencies with good online reviews, verify credentials, and avoid deals that seem too good to be true.

soloFemale.overview: Da Nang is considered one of the safest cities in Vietnam for solo female travelers, ranking among the top destinations in Southeast Asia. Women report feeling comfortable walking alone during the day and in well-lit tourist areas at night. The city has low harassment rates, respectful local culture, and strong security presence in tourist zones.
tips: Avoid dark alleyways and isolated areas late at night; stick to main roads and well-lit neighborhoods
Use ride-hailing apps like Grab instead of flagging down street taxis for safer transportation
Choose accommodation in tourist-friendly areas like My Khe Beach or An Thuong with good lighting and 24/7 reception
Dress modestly when visiting temples and local areas; cover shoulders and knees to show respect
Share your location with trusted contacts when exploring remote areas or beaches alone

nightSafety.overview: Da Nang maintains good safety standards at night, particularly around popular areas like the Han River, My Khe Beach, and downtown districts. The city has well-lit streets, visible police patrols, and a thriving nightlife scene that operates safely until late hours. However, some residential areas may have poor lighting.
tips: Stay in well-populated tourist areas with good lighting such as An Thuong and the riverside district
Use reputable transportation like Grab or licensed taxis rather than walking alone in quiet areas
Choose busy restaurants and bars for evening activities; avoid secluded venues
Keep valuables secure and avoid displaying expensive items in crowded nightlife areas
Be aware that clubs typically close around 2 AM; plan transportation in advance

transport.metro: Da Nang does not have a metro system. Public buses are available but have limited English signage and can be overcrowded.
transport.rideshare: Grab is the dominant ride-hailing service and is considered very safe with GPS tracking, upfront pricing, and driver verification. Both GrabCar and GrabBike (motorbike) options are available 24/7.
transport.taxis: Licensed taxi companies like Mai Linh (green cars) and Vinasun (white cars) are reliable when using the meter. Airport pickup may involve surcharges of 15,000-25,000 VND.
transport.tips: Download Grab app before arriving for seamless transportation. Always wear helmets on GrabBike rides (provided by driver). Traffic is calmer than Hanoi or Ho Chi Minh City but still requires caution when crossing streets.

customs: Remove shoes when entering homes and temples; look for shoe racks as indicators
Dress modestly in religious sites and local areas; cover shoulders and knees
Greet with 'Xin chao' and a slight bow or handshake; wait to be offered a handshake
Avoid touching someone's head or pointing feet toward people, especially in temples
Use both hands when giving or receiving business cards and gifts as a sign of respect

health.overview: Da Nang has several international-standard private hospitals and clinics that provide quality healthcare with English-speaking staff. Public hospitals are more affordable but may have language barriers and longer wait times.
health.water: Tap water is not safe to drink. Use bottled water for drinking and brushing teeth. Most restaurants and hotels provide safe bottled water.
health.vaccinations: No specific vaccinations required for entry. Consider hepatitis A & B, typhoid, and Japanese encephalitis for extended stays. Routine vaccines should be up to date.
health.altitude: Da Nang is at sea level with tropical climate. High humidity year-round. Typhoon season runs October-December with heavy rains and strong winds.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Consulate General Ho Chi Minh City: +84 28 3520 4200 (Da Nang area is covered by this office). US Embassy Hanoi: +84 24 3850 5000 for major emergencies.

faq.q: Is Da Nang safe for tourists?
faq.a: Yes, Da Nang is considered one of Vietnam's safest cities for tourists with low crime rates, friendly locals, and strong tourism infrastructure. The US State Department maintains a Level 1 advisory recommending normal precautions.

faq.q: Is Da Nang safe at night?
faq.a: Da Nang is generally safe at night, especially in tourist areas like My Khe Beach, An Thuong, and the Han River district. These areas have good lighting, police patrols, and active nightlife until around 2 AM.

faq.q: Is Da Nang safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Yes, Da Nang is considered one of the safest destinations in Southeast Asia for solo female travelers. Women report feeling comfortable during the day and in well-lit areas at night, with low harassment rates and respectful local culture.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Da Nang?
faq.a: There are no particularly dangerous areas, but avoid poorly lit alleyways at night and be cautious on winding mountain roads in Son Tra Peninsula. Stick to main tourist areas like My Khe Beach and An Thuong for the safest experience.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Da Nang?
faq.a: Grab ride-hailing is the safest and most convenient option. Licensed taxis are reliable when metered. Public buses are safe but may have language barriers. GrabBike (motorbike taxi) is popular and safe when wearing helmets.
